steps.title: Identify the persistent motion and defiant character
steps.content: The phrase “Ever repulsed, yet ever rushing on” uses the contrast word “yet” to show continuous forward motion despite being driven back by the rocks. This is reinforced by “Filled with a life that will not know defeat”—the waves explicitly refuse to be stopped.

steps.title: Match the characterization to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined portion characterizes the waves as relentless and enduring—they persist despite resistance and refuse defeat. A incorrectly describes the scene as “intimidating” when the speaker feels delight; C and D introduce concepts absent from the text.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
